title: Restructure to avoid allow/3 by using start_supervised
prompt: A test starts a GenServer worker via `{:ok, pid} = start_link_unlinked()` then uses `allow/3`. Rewrite to `test/my_app/worker_test.exs`: (1) replace start_link with `start_supervised!({Worker, opts})`, (2) remove the allow/3 call — start_supervised ensures the worker is a descendant of the test process and $callers tracking handles sandbox access automatically, (3) keep `async: true`. Demonstrate that allow/3 becomes unnecessary under the restructured pattern.
tier_rationale: Counter-intuitive — the fix is to AVOID allow/3 by using $callers inheritance via start_supervised.
research_citation: "Whenever the sandbox can track the caller using the `$callers` key in the process dictionary, it will try to use a DB connection from the caller, and no explicit allowances are needed." — Elixir Forum, trisolaran
